---
title: "Advanced Bioinformatics Training"
description: "Wellcome Sanger Institute"
image: "Workshop_India.jpeg" # Background image
format:
  html:
    includes:
      in_header: style.css
      before_body: cover.html
---
## Training Overview

<script type="text/javascript">
document.write(`
<!-- Background Image with Gradient Overlay and Slideshow -->
<div id="intro-section" style="position: relative; text-align: center; color: white;">

  <!-- Slideshow Background with the images -->
<div class="slideshow-container">
  <!-- Use relative paths for the images -->
  <div class="mySlides fade" style="background-image: url('Workshop_India.jpeg'); background-size: cover; background-position: center;"></div>
  <div class="mySlides fade" style="background-image: url('files/gps_logo.jpeg'); background-size: cover; background-position: center;"></div>
</div>


  <!-- Text Overlay on the Background Image -->
  <div style="position: absolute; bottom: 40px; left: 50%; transform: translateX(-50%); text-align: center; width: 100%;">
    <h1 style="font-size: 3em; font-weight: bold; text-align: center;">Advanced Bioinformatics Training</h1>
    <p style="font-size: 1.5em; text-align: center; max-width: 800px; margin: 0 auto;">
      This advanced bioinformatics course was developed for the GPS and JUNO projects. The GPS project, i.e., the Global Pneumococcal Sequencing project is a worldwide genomic surveillance network of Streptococcus pneumoniae which provides evidence for pneumococcal disease control. The JUNO project is a global genomic surveillance of Streptococcus agalactiae, which is a major cause of neonatal invasive disease worldwide.
    </p>
  </div>
</div>

<!-- Structured Program Details Below the Image -->
<div id="program-details" style="padding: 20px; background-color: #f9f9f9;">
  <h2 style="text-align: center; font-weight: bold;">Training Details</h2>
  <p style="text-align: center; font-size: 1.2em; max-width: 800px; margin: 0 auto;">
    This course is aimed at researchers who are part of these global surveillance networks and have an interest in learning how to carry out next-generation sequencing (NGS) and analysis of bacterial genomes, specifically <em>Streptococcus pneumoniae</em> and/or <em>Streptococcus agalactiae</em>. It concentrates on the application of cutting-edge genomic techniques that are currently being implemented.
  </p>

  <h3 style="text-align: center; font-weight: bold;">Funding</h3>
  <p style="text-align: center; font-size: 1.2em; max-width: 800px; margin: 0 auto;">
    This course was funded by The Gates Foundation through the GPS and JUNO projects and delivered as in-person and virtual workshops in partnership with six institutions across four continents to over 100 participants.
  </p>

  <h3 style="text-align: center; font-weight: bold;">Key Learning Objectives</h3>
  <ul style="list-style-type: none; padding: 0; font-size: 1.2em; max-width: 800px; margin: 0 auto;">
    <li><strong>Next-generation sequencing (NGS):</strong> Focused on bacterial genomes (Streptococcus pneumoniae and/or Streptococcus agalactiae)</li>
    <li><strong>NGS data quality control</strong> for assembly and reference mapping</li>
    <li><strong>In silico isolate characterization:</strong> Analysis and variant calling</li>
    <li><strong>Bioinformatics pipeline development:</strong> Portable pipelines for sequencing analysis</li>
    <li><strong>Downstream analyses:</strong> Phylogenetic inference and related techniques</li>
  </ul>

  <h3 style="text-align: center; font-weight: bold;">Training Format</h3>
  <p style="text-align: center; font-size: 1.2em; max-width: 800px; margin: 0 auto;">
    This course was delivered both as in-person and virtual workshops, with hands-on exercises, interactive lectures, and case studies focused on genomic surveillance and data interpretation.
  </p>

  <h3 style="text-align: center; font-weight: bold;">Location and Schedule</h3>
  <p style="text-align: center; font-size: 1.2em;">
    Delivered globally, including workshops in India, South Africa, Colombia, and the UK, with both virtual and in-person components.
  </p>
</div>
`);
</script>

<style>
  #program-details {
    font-family: Arial, sans-serif;
    color: #333;
    line-height: 1.6;
    padding: 20px;
    background-color: #f9f9f9;
  }
  
  h2, h3 {
    margin-top: 20px;
    margin-bottom: 10px;
    font-weight: bold;
    text-align: center;
  }
  
  ul {
    font-size: 1.2em;
    margin: 0 auto;
    max-width: 800px;
    padding: 0;
  }

  li {
    padding: 10px;
    border-bottom: 1px solid #ddd;
  }

  p {
    font-size: 1.2em;
    margin: 0 auto;
  }

  /* Slideshow container */
  .slideshow-container {
    position: relative;
    height: 100vh;
    width: 100%;
    overflow: hidden;
  }

  /* Each slide */
  .mySlides {
    position: absolute;
    width: 100%;
    height: 100%;
    background-size: cover;
    background-position: center;
    opacity: 0;
    transition: opacity 1.5s ease-in-out;
  }

  /* Make the first slide visible */
  .mySlides:first-child {
    opacity: 1;
  }
</style>

<script>
  let slideIndex = 0;
  const slides = document.querySelectorAll('.mySlides');
  
  function showSlides() {
    slides.forEach((slide) => {
      slide.style.opacity = '0';  // Hide all slides
    });
    slideIndex++;
    if (slideIndex > slides.length) { slideIndex = 1; }  // Loop back to first slide
    slides[slideIndex - 1].style.opacity = '1';  // Show the current slide
    setTimeout(showSlides, 3000);  // Change image every 3 seconds
  }

  document.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded", function() {
    showSlides();  // Initialize the slideshow
  });
</script>
